intended_use: Reads structured data files from a per-run sandboxed workspace, performs deterministic analysis, and writes result reports back to the workspace. Operates entirely without network egress.
out_of_scope_uses: Generating code that will be executed outside the sandbox
Producing medical, legal, or financial advice for end users
Processing data classified above 'restricted' tier without dual-control approval
Any tool call requiring outbound network access
known_limitations: Model may hallucinate numeric values; consumers must independently verify aggregate metrics
No access to external knowledge sources at runtime — analyses bounded to provided workspace files
Token budget capped at 50,000 per run; very large files require pre-chunking by the caller

nist_ai_rmf_map.govern: GV-1.1, GV-1.2 — capability manifest + signed identity envelope
nist_ai_rmf_map.map: MP-2.3, MP-3.4 — intended-use scoping + risk classification
nist_ai_rmf_map.measure: MS-1.1, MS-2.5, MS-3.2 — anomaly scoring + audit telemetry
nist_ai_rmf_map.manage: MG-1.3, MG-4.1 — kill switches + DSAR/incident response

evaluation_summary: Pre-release: passed sandbox path-traversal suite (test_sandbox.py), policy denial suite (test_policy.py), audit redaction (test_audit_redaction.py), governance controls (test_governance_controls.py). Review cadence: quarterly.
